title: elaeodendron aquifolium
intro: Elaeodendron aquifolium, also known as the Australian Olive or Quoin Tree, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its elliptical leaves and white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Elaeodendron aquifolium include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality. Elaeodendron aquifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of medicinal applications and traditional uses.
